task: 2.2 - driver end-to-end (grafos ONNX + splat CPU) con parity total
credits: 98mxr/GMFSS_Fortuna (MIT) -- softsplat_torch.py y arquitectura de referencia del pipeline
HolyWu/vs-gmfss_fortuna (MIT) -- FeatureNet, MetricNet, FusionNet_b (GridNet), GMFlow, pesos *_base.pkl

resolution.note: Graphs are traced/exported at this exact shape (no dynamic_axes) -- see toolkit/export_components.py. driver.pipeline.GmfssDriver.interpolate_pair operates ONLY at this fixed padded resolution and raises ValueError otherwise. Padding/cropping arbitrary input resolutions to a multiple of 64 (matching toolkit/gmfss_pg_pipeline.target_padded_size) is explicitly out of scope for this driver -- that is Phase 4's job when the driver is wired into Upflow's real video pipeline.

input_normalization: uint8 RGB HWC, /255.0 -> float32 CHW [0,1], batch dim 1. driver.pipeline does NOT perform this conversion itself -- callers must pass already-normalized+padded float32 [1,3,1088,1920] tensors, matching refs/golden/<pair>/input_norm_img{0,1}.npy exactly (see toolkit/gmfss_pg_pipeline.normalize_frame).

featurenet.note: Called once per source image (img0, img1) inside reuse() -- full padded resolution in, half/quarter/eighth-resolution feature pyramid out. FeatureNet does its own internal stride-2 downsampling; it does NOT take img_half as input.

gmflow.note: Single graph, called twice per pair inside reuse() with swapped args: (img0_half, img1_half) -> flow01, (img1_half, img0_half) -> flow10. attn_splits_list=[2,8], corr_radius_list=[-1,4], prop_radius_list=[-1,1], pred_bidir_flow=False are baked into the traced graph (see toolkit/export_components.py GMFlowExportWrapper).

metricnet.note: Called once per pair inside reuse(), after both GMFlow calls. DirectML sessions for this graph MUST set graph_optimization_level = ORT_DISABLE_ALL -- the default fused DML kernel reproducibly hung the GPU (DXGI_ERROR_DEVICE_HUNG) on the Task 1.1 validation hardware. See toolkit/validate_ort.py make_session().

fusionnet.note: Called once per requested timestep. fusion_rgb = concat([img0_half, splat_I1t, splat_I2t, img1_half], channel dim) -- 4x3=12 channels. fusion_featN = concat([splat_feat1tN, splat_feat2tN], channel dim). GridNet upsamples internally: half-res inputs -> full padded-res output. Driver clamps raw_out to [0,1] to get the final frame (matches refs/golden/<pair>/final_frame_padded.npy).

splat_calls.impl: driver.softsplat.splat_softmax (Task 2.1), strMode='soft' equivalent -- softmax-weighted forward warp, CPU-only (numpy in/out, torch-CPU internally). Never runs on DML: DirectML rejects ScatterND with reduction='add' outright, see driver/softsplat.py module docstring.

pyramid_levels.feature_source: feat_scale1 (H/2,W/2 of full padded res == img_half res)
pyramid_levels.flow_metric_resize: none -- F1t/F2t/Z1t/Z2t are already at img_half resolution

bilinear resize flow by 0.5 THEN multiply by 0.5 (a flow vector's magnitude is in pixels of its own resolution, so it must shrink with the resize); metric resized by 0.5 WITHOUT rescale (it is a log-weight, not a displacement)

timestep_weighting.note: Pure scalar weighting, same code path for any timestep in [0,1]. Golden reference data (refs/golden/) was captured at a single timestep=0.5 only (see refs/golden/meta.json) -- validate_driver.py's parity gate therefore targets timesteps=[0.5]; GmfssDriver.interpolate_pair supporting other timestep values is exercised by tests/test_pipeline.py's fake-graph unit tests, not by golden-data parity (no golden data exists at other t).

run_graph_contract: run_graph(name: str, feeds: dict[str, np.ndarray]) -> list[np.ndarray], outputs in the order documented under graphs.<name>.outputs above. Injected into GmfssDriver(assets, run_graph) -- production callers wrap onnxruntime.InferenceSession.run(None, feeds); tests inject fakes (see tests/test_pipeline.py).
splat_backend_contract: GmfssDriver(assets, run_graph, splat_fn=None) -- splat_fn=None (default) uses driver.softsplat.splat_softmax (CPU, numpy/torch-CPU). Pass splat_fn=driver.softsplat_cl.splat_softmax to opt into the OpenCL GPU backend (Task 3.1/3.2); same contract, falls back to CPU automatically with a one-time warning if no working OpenCL GPU is present. See driver/pipeline.py's SplatFn Protocol.

fp16_variants.note: Task 3.2 attempted onnxconverter-common fp16 conversion (keep_io_types=True mixed precision, see toolkit/convert_fp16.py) on all 4 graphs. Only fusionnet converts to a graph onnxruntime can actually load -- featurenet/metricnet/gmflow hit a reproducible onnxconverter-common bug (a value that is both a declared graph output and consumed by further internal computation gets a stale/conflicting dtype declaration onnxruntime rejects at load time). Documented, not forced through -- see README.md fp16 section and .superpowers/sdd/task-3.2-report.md.

fusionnet.validated_rel_err_dml: 0.0027-0.0041 (3 golden cases, well under this repo's existing 1e-2 DirectML tolerance convention)
fusionnet.end_to_end_effect: Also measurably reduces GMFlow's own per-call DirectML latency when all 4 sessions coexist in one process (see README's GMFlow multi-session-contention section) -- a smaller fusionnet session footprint appears to reduce cross-session GPU resource contention, not just fusionnet's own compute time.
